page.title: Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands

revision.text:
[[ja:北海道音楽大行進]] {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands |English Name=Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands |Type=Music |image=文館.JPG |image Size=230px |image Caption=Starting point for the children’s section, {{ill|Asahikawa Civic Culture Hall|ja|旭川市民文化会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa Civic Culture Hall|1=Asahikawa Civic Culture Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511736}} |Nickname= |Official Name=Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands |Former Name={{ill|Hokkaido Gokoku Shrine|ja|北海道護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道護國神社|1=Hokkaidō Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11403068}} Reitai-sai Memorial Music Grand Parade |Date Held=First Saturday of June each year |First Held=June 5, 1929 (Showa 4) |Last Held= |Event Hours=11:00 - 17:30
(including after-concert) |Venue=[[Asahibashi (Asahikawa City)|Riverine Asahikawa Park]]
[[Heiwa Street Shopping Park]] and other locations |Organizers=[[Asahikawa City]]
Asahikawa City Board of Education
Asahikawa District Wind Band Federation
Asahikawa District Elementary School Band Federation
[[Hokkaido Shimbun]] Asahikawa Bureau
Asahikawa Tourism Association |Co-Organizers=Hokkaido Cultural Foundation |Sponsors=[[Hokkaido Regional Development Bureau]] Asahikawa Development and Construction Department
[[Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau]]
Hokkaido Educational Bureau, Kamikawa Branch
Asahikawa Chamber of Commerce
Asahikawa Citizens’ Committee Coordination Association |Collaborators=Yamaha Music Retailing Asahikawa Store |Operator=Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands Executive Committee |Producer= |Participants=90 groups (2015) |Visitors=153,000 (2015) |Access Name= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus= |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} {{nihongo|Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands|北海道音楽大行進}} is a marching band festival held annually in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|qid=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, Japan, in June. Formerly known as the "Memorial Music Grand Parade" and "Gokoku Shrine Dedication Music Parade," it is celebrated as one of Japan's largest and world-class festivals in scale{{Citation needed|date=February 2, 2023}}[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d-lJXktOHHk Asahikawa City Library Channel – Photos of the Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands].https://www.city.asahikawa.hokkaido.jp/foreign/d059614.html == Overview == Participants donning colorful costumes march through the city center, performing music. After the parade, an after-concert is held at the [[Heiwa Street Shopping Park]]. Initially intended as a memorial event, it has evolved into a festival widely embraced by the community. Participation has grown over the years. During the 81st event in 2013 (held on June 8), 106 groups (83 teams) joined, attracting participants not only from Asahikawa but also from cities across Hokkaido, such as {{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|qid=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, {{ill|Iwamizawa|en|Iwamizawa|qid=Q693264|lt=Iwamizawa|1=Iwamizawa}}, {{ill|Furano, Hokkaido|en|Furano, Hokkaido|qid=Q654738|lt=Furano|1=Furano, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Kitami|en|Kitami|qid=Q265555|lt=Kitami|1=Kitami}}, and {{ill|Abashiri|en|Abashiri|qid=Q305640|lt=Abashiri|1=Abashiri}}. With over 160,000 spectators, the festival is a hallmark of early summer in Asahikawa. == History == *1929 – (Showa 4) Held for the first time on June 5 during the Reitai-sai of the {{ill|Hokkaido Gokoku Shrine|ja|北海道護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道護國神社|1=Hokkaidō Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11403068}}, with 10 groups and 200 participants. *1948 – (Showa 23, 20th parade) Briefly suspended after this year. *1953 – (Showa 28, 21st parade) Resumed. *1974 – (Showa 49, 42nd parade) Renamed to "Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands." *1994 – (Heisei 6, 62nd parade) Route changed; the opening ceremony was held at Riverine Asahikawa Park, and the parade began at Asahikawa Tokiwa Rotary. *1999 – (Heisei 11, 67th parade) After the closure of Hokkai Times, an executive committee comprising Asahikawa City and the Hokkaido Shimbun took over. *2002 – (Heisei 14, 70th parade) The date was changed to the first Saturday of June. *2016 – (Heisei 28, 84th parade) Canceled due to bad weather for the first time in history. *2020 – (Reiwa 2, 88th parade) Canceled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. *2022 – (Reiwa 4, 90th parade) Held after a three-year hiatus. *2023 – (Reiwa 5, 91st parade) Held in rainy weather; the mounted band led the parade. The after-concert was canceled. *2024 – (Reiwa 6, 92nd parade) The after-concert was held again after a two-year gap. == Participating Groups == *2006 (74th parade) – 96 groups (78 teams), 4,200 participants. *2007 (75th parade) – 97 groups, 4,300 participants. *2008 (76th parade) – 89 groups, 4,096 participants. *2013 (81st parade) – 106 groups, 3,735 participants. *2024 (92nd parade) – 81 groups, approximately 2,800 participants. == Route == During the first event, the parade route spanned from the Hokkai Times Asahikawa Bureau to {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|qid=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station|1=Asahikawa Station}}, through {{ill|Heiwa Street Shopping Park|ja|平和通買物公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiwa Street Shopping Park|1=Heiwa-dōri Kaimono Kōen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482272}} (then known as "Shidan Street"), across {{ill|Asahibashi|ja|旭橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahibashi|1=asahibashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511820}}, and concluded at {{ill|Hokkaido Gokoku Shrine|ja|北海道護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道護國神社|1=Hokkaidō Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11403068}}. The route has been adjusted in subsequent years to accommodate larger participation. == Television Broadcast == Local coverage by {{ill|Asahikawa Cable Television|ja|旭川ケーブルテレビ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa Cable Television|1=Asahikawa Cable Television|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511690}} features two-hour edited versions aired in the weeks following the event. Historically, the event was also broadcast in a condensed one-hour format by {{ill|Sapporo Television Broadcasting|en|Sapporo Television Broadcasting|qid=Q843629|lt=STV|1=Sapporo Television Broadcasting}} for the Hokkaido region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://asahikawafc.jp/locanavi/show.php?template=item.template.asta.html&item_id=90 Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands] - Introduction by Asahikawa Tourism Association *[http://www.hat.hi-ho.ne.jp/asa-suiren/parade/ongkousi.htm Records of the Hokkaido Grand Parade of Marching Bands] {{translated page|ja|北海道音楽大行進|version=101623812|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北海道音楽大行進]] {{wikidata link|Q11403725}} [[Category:1929 establishments]] [[Category:Culture of Asahikawa]] [[Category:Culture of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Events in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Festivals in Hokkaido]] [[Category:June]] [[Category:Music festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Music of Hokkaido prefecture]] [[Category:Recurring events established in 1929]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
